title: Headhunter Company

{{Quote|Headhunter Company, you'll follow Jaster to the recovery site. Kill anything that gets in your way.|[[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]]|<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />}}
'''Headhunter Company''' was a [[Division/Legends|division]] of [[True Mandalorians|True Mandalorian]] [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] under the command<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> of [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|''Mand'alor'']] [[Jaster Mereel]]<ref name="HotM">{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}}</ref> during the [[Battle of Korda Six|mission]] to [[Korda Six]] to rescue pinned-down [[Korda Defense Force|Kordan Defense Force]] personnel from hostile territory. A heavy [[Infantry/Legends|infantry]] unit, Headhunter [[Company/Legends|Company]] was tasked with recovering the Kordan security team from their besieged position and eliminating any [[Kordan|hostile natives]] in their way.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

Despite intelligence reports denoting minimal resistance to be expected, the native Kordans assaulted the True Mandalorian [[Mercenary/Legends|mercenaries]] in force as they made [[Planet/Legends|planetfall]]. As [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett's]] [[Jango's grunts|troops]] came under heavy [[Blaster/Legends|fire]], Mereel ordered Headhunter Company and [[Vertigo Company]] to reinforce their Mandalorian comrades; Vertigo's [[Commander/Legends|commander]], [[Montross (bounty hunter)|Montross]], refused and instead pressed his attack on the Kordan natives, only to get [[Jetpack/Legends|shot down]] and ultimately rescued by Mereel. Mereel ordered all True Mandalorian units to retreat, including Headhunter Company, but the entire operation on Korda Six was soon revealed to be a trap laid out by the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]],<ref name="OpenSeasons2" /> a renegade Mandalorian splinter-faction in sworn opposition to the True Mandalorians.<ref name="HotM" /> The Death Watch's [[Secret Mandalore|leader]], [[Tor Vizsla]],<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Death/Legends|slew]] Mereel on the field of battle, and Jango Fett was chosen to succeed him as leader of the Mandalorians. The True Mandalorians were forced to withdraw, and Headhunter Company dispersed back into the larger ranks of their forces.<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />

==Behind the scenes==
Headhunter Company was first introduced to ''[[Star Wars]]'' in the [[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|second issue]] of the [[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series, written by [[W. Haden Blackman|Hayden Blackman]] and published [[June 17]], [[2002]].<ref name="OpenSeasons2" />
